blocker: The v16 contract is not yet internally authoritative: the active F-28 fallback clause still permits heuristic recovery after route-record failure, child title scheduling is both required and forbidden, title-length rules conflict, and the current Next section still instructs superseded v6/v10 work.

evidence: prd.md:197 explicitly moves the former wide-only NOW row and primary-row context gauge to the F-37 all-layout subordinate line.
prd.md:344 explicitly removes wide primary-row and narrow/stack inline context telemetry.
prd.md:371-377 defines exactly one detail row after identity and before the sub-agent strip for wide/narrow/stack, including live/missing/stale/dead truth-table cases and context-first clipping.
prd.md:389 acceptance pins wide@168, wide@120, narrow@100, stack@60, row order, CJK-safe clipping, and terminal-width bounds.
prd.md:343 retires the title-only PID join and assigns title/NOW/context to one association.

prd.md:379 requires exact (pid,proc_start), allows only one same-harness realpath(cwd) fallback when exact identity is absent, rejects PID reuse, duplicate PID, cwd ambiguity, and cross-harness matching, and forbids parent-context inheritance.
prd.md:390 requires fixtures for joint value adoption, differing parent/child percentages, PID reuse, duplicate PID, cwd ambiguity, and cross-harness refusal.

prd.md:320 and prd.md:362-367 correctly specify that any explicit invalid/conflicting route tuple remains unknown/ambiguous, cannot fall through to artifacts, and that registry-only evidence cannot fabricate topology, progress, gate passage, or node completion.

prd.md:303 remains active normative text and still says route-record absence or parse failure falls back to the current pipe heuristic without conditioning that fallback on complete route-tuple absence.

Because prd.md:303 is not marked historical or explicitly superseded, an implementer can conform to it while violating the v16 fail-closed rule; the earlier RF-03 supersession requirement is therefore not fully closed.

prd.md:369 defines artifact inference only when the route tuple is wholly absent and exactly one exact plan directory exists; it permits stage_label only and forbids route, node, progress, gate, and completion synthesis.
prd.md:393 separately pins exactly-one, zero, and multiple artifact candidates, including ambiguity evidence for multiple candidates.
prd.md:392 pins an invalid explicit route tuple plus plausible artifact as unknown/ambiguous rather than artifact-derived.

prd.md:228-231 and prd.md:383 make F-39 the current numeric source of truth: default concurrency 3, hard max 4, default/hard start budget 4 per rolling 60 seconds, and 600/150-second main/child debounces.

prd.md:383 requires one shared cross-process pool and locks, stale-lease recovery, both kill switches, default-provider tool denial, shell-free custom argv, and wrapper-owned custom no-tools enforcement.
prd.md:549 revises the historical F-23 lock to the same limits, while prd.md:398-399 pins hermetic quota and zero-provider tests.
prd.md:381 makes only missing, stale, malformed, or source-sequence-regressed telemetry unknown and explicitly allows a numeric decrease caused by compaction.
prd.md:397 repeats the permitted numeric decrease and rejects only source-sequence regression in the acceptance matrix.
The component-local v14 snapshot and authoritative aggregate v14 copy are byte-identical with SHA-256 5b4ccb4a9ff7b7442d76179cd5320b1f8b4476c69f7670c36e0e9663ef8dabc4.

The v15 snapshot SHA-256 is 5b0097064c38f705054797f2af29007baf2251ec1eb66b68296d9f771a7ddf57, matching pipeline_summary.md:23; its v14-to-v15 diff contains the declared sync/migrate add, literal actor=sync, logical-cwd, no-op, and no-backfill delta.
pipeline_state.yaml:11-16 and pipeline_summary.md:5,17-38 declare v16 spec done, v16 development pending, resume phase dev/F-36~F-39, and the carried v15 memory-sync delta.
prd.md:360 assigns source-agnostic resolution to projection.py and prohibits render/plain/JSON from independently calling live_stage().
prd.md:449-451 places projection.py and WorkProjection in the module tree between evidence models and rendering.
prd.md:480-485 shows Session/DispatchJob evidence flowing through one WorkProjection resolver to group/process/plain rendering and additive JSON.

checks.issue: The older F-28 consumer clause still allows pipe-heuristic fallback on record absence or parse failure, while current v16 permits fallback only when the entire explicit tuple is absent.
checks.required_resolution: Edit the F-28 clause itself to state the v16 route-tuple boundary or label it historical and explicitly superseded by F-28a/F-36.

checks.issue: F-17/F-23/F-39 include normal dispatch children in title/NOW scheduling with a 150-second debounce, but F-24 still excludes every general loop/cron/dispatch worker from the main title scheduler.
checks.required_resolution: Define one eligibility predicate: exclude only mem/title/app-server and other explicitly internal workers while allowing the intended normal dispatch child population, or state a narrower child class and test it.

checks.issue: The current provider output is simultaneously 4-8 words/64 characters, 3-6 words/40 characters, and in the historical lock/current Next text up to 12 words/96 characters.
checks.required_resolution: Choose one current TITLE validation contract, mark earlier sizes explicitly historical/superseded, and make F-17, F-22, locked decisions, and Next agree.

checks.issue: The current Next section starts with the correct v16 cycle but then keeps a pending v6 F-22/F-23 12-word/96-character task, a pending v10 implementation cycle already described as complete elsewhere, and the obsolete v2 command and sequence.
checks.required_resolution: Make Next contain only the current v16 autopilot-code handoff; move completed or superseded sequences to an explicitly historical section.

explicitly_historical_and_nonblocking: Pipeline-summary v6/v8/v10 implementation narratives are version-labeled history.
The v14 and v15 snapshot records are immutable history and agree with their recorded hashes/deltas.
stale_active_or_ambiguous: F-28 at prd.md:303 is still phrased as a live consumer contract.
F-24 at prd.md:233 is still phrased as a live scheduler exclusion.
The v6 F-22 locked decision at prd.md:548 has no explicit supersession for title size.
The single current Next heading at prd.md:590 contains obsolete, non-completed instructions.

implementation_readiness.assessment: The v16 design is otherwise detailed enough to implement: it names projection.py ownership, additive model/JSON fields, source precedence, ambiguity codes, layout truth tables, DAG behavior, quota constants, and hermetic verification. It is not safe to hand to autopilot-code until the four authority conflicts above are removed, because different compliant implementations would schedule different children, validate different title sizes, and disagree on invalid route fallback.
ready_elements: Common WorkProjection schema and group/process/plain/JSON consumers are named.
Exact, registry-only, artifact-only, and ambiguity boundaries are testable.
Arbitrary composed DAG, parallel sibling, fan-in, width/overflow, additive JSON, context orthogonality, quota, and no-provider fixtures are specified.
The version transaction and dev-pending state are consistent.
implementation_readiness.completion_gate: Reconcile IC-01 through IC-04 in the canonical PRD and rerun this independent final review before starting autopilot-code.

The accepted route file independently seals unit_catalog_digest/composed and node inputs/outputs/write_scope/completion gate.

hermetic_quota_limits.evidence: prd.md:383,398 specifies fake clock, isolated state root, 200-session backlog, 3/4 concurrency, four starts per rolling 60 seconds, fifth-start refusal, window recovery, and direct-runner lease enforcement.

interactive_registered_projection_parity.evidence: prd.md:360,391 requires equivalent exact Session and DispatchJob evidence to produce identical normalized route/node/state/progress across all surfaces.

zero_live_provider_invocation.evidence: prd.md:383,399 makes JSON/once/demo/test no-spawn and requires fail-if-reached subprocess guards for both default and custom provider paths. No live provider was invoked during this review.

unsupported_runtime_contracts: The spec-read marker side effect could not write under /home/Uihyeop/agent_setting/.spec-grounding because that filesystem is read-only; the PRD itself was read completely.

No live default or custom title provider was invoked. Provider quotas, costs, and external behavior were not empirically measured; only hermetic repository tests and specification boundaries were reviewed.

The v15 snapshot digest matches the transaction summary and its delta from authoritative v14 was inspected, but the pre-transaction mutable v15 file no longer exists as a second independent byte source outside the sealed snapshot; no stronger replay claim is made.

The selected baseline suite predates v16 implementation. It cannot supply runtime proof for WorkProjection, new layout rows, 3/4 quota behavior, or provider zero-call acceptance until the subsequent autopilot-code cycle adds those tests.
